channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇴🇲 Iranian drones strike Oman oil storage facility at Salalah port
item.description: Iran expanded attacks beyond the Strait of Hormuz, hitting fuel storage tanks at Oman's Port of Salalah — a key alternative route for tankers bypassing the blocked strait. The strike marks an escalation in the campaign targeting regional energy infrastructure. | Source: Oil Price | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump dismisses Iran's 14-point peace proposal as insufficient
item.description: Trump said he would review Iran's new peace plan but warned Tehran "has not yet paid a big enough price" for 47 years of actions. Iran's proposal, submitted via Islamabad, includes ending the conflict and a new Strait of Hormuz framework. | Source: Daily Sabah | Category: US-Iran War

item.title: 🛢️ Oil surges past $120/barrel as Trump rejects Iran overture
item.description: Brent crude hit its highest level of the conflict after Trump maintained the naval blockade of Iranian ports. The UAE announced it would leave OPEC on May 1 to respond to market shortages without cartel constraints. | Source: Al-Monitor | Category: Energy

item.title: 🤖 Pentagon's Office of Net Assessment chief joins Anthropic
item.description: James Baker, who led the Defense Department's long-term strategic analysis office from 2015-2025, joined AI company Anthropic as a "strategist-in-residence." The move comes after the Trump administration temporarily shuttered ONA and designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk. | Source: Defense One | Category: Technology

item.title: 🇨🇳 China stages naval drill east of Philippines amid US-Balikatan exercises
item.description: The PLA Southern Theatre Command conducted drills led by Type 055 destroyer Zunyi in waters east of Luzon, calling it a "necessary action" in response to the regional situation. The exercises coincide with the 19-day Balikatan 2026 joint drills between US and Philippine forces. | Source: SCMP | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 💰 Fed Kashkari: Iran War Limits Rate Guidance
item.description: Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari warned that prolonged Iran war increases inflation and economic damage risks, limiting how much guidance the central bank should provide on rate policy. | Source: Reuters | Category: Economy

item.title: 🚢 Trump Announces "Project Freedom" to Free Ships from Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump announced the US will launch a humanitarian effort on Monday to guide stranded ships out of the Iran-blocked Strait of Hormuz, calling it a "humanitarian gesture" amid ongoing conflict. | Source: Reuters | Category: US-Iran War

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Seeks Advantage Between Trump and Iran as War Evolves
item.description: With Trump visiting Beijing looming, China is pushing Iran to negotiate while its companies continue exporting materials that could aid Irans military, walking a delicate diplomatic line. | Source: New York Times | Category: US-China

item.title: ⛪ Secretary of State Rubio to Meet Pope Leo This Week
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to Italy to meet Pope Leo, as Trumps criticism of the pontiff draws political backlash. The visit comes amid tensions between the administration and the Vatican. | Source: Reuters | Category: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK to Join EU 105 Billion Dollar Ukraine Loan
item.description: Britain will enter talks to join the European Unions £78 billion loan to Ukraine, deepening European defense ties as US pressure mounts. Prime Minister Starmer aims to strengthen support for Kyiv. | Source: Reuters | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇺🇸🇲🇦 Two US Troops Missing During African Lion Military Exercise in Morocco
item.description: Two U.S. service members participating in the African Lion joint military exercises were reported missing near Tan Tan in southern Morocco. AFRICOM and Moroccan forces are conducting search operations. | Source: Reuters | Category: Military

item.title: 💨🇺🇸 Trump Administration Halts US Wind Farm Projects Citing National Security
item.description: President Trump's administration has brought U.S. onshore wind development to a halt, citing national security concerns. The Financial Times reports the move affects multiple pending projects. | Source: Reuters | Category: Energy

item.title: 🇮🇱🚢 Israel Court Extends Detention of Gaza Flotilla Activists
item.description: An Israeli court extended by two days the detention of two activists arrested aboard a Gaza-bound flotilla intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters near Greece. | Source: Reuters | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇪🇺📉 ECB Policymaker Warns Euro Zone Recession Concerns Are Real and Justified
item.description: Bank of Greece governor Yannis Stournaras warned that concerns the euro zone could slip into recession if the Middle East conflict continues are "real and justified," with Iran war talks key for ECB policy. | Source: Reuters | Category: Economy

item.title: 🌏⛏️ ADB Launches Finance Scheme for Critical Mineral Supply Chains in Asia-Pacific
item.description: The Asian Development Bank launched a new financing scheme to help countries in Asia and the Pacific develop supply chains for critical minerals used in clean energy, batteries, electric vehicles, and digital technology. | Source: Reuters | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🏛️ Top Senate Armed Services Democrat: No timeouts on Iran war powers
item.description: Sen. Jack Reed (D-RI) criticized Trump on the 60-day War Powers deadline, stating there are no timeouts in constitutional war powers requirements as the administration continues military action against Iran. | Source: The Hill | Category: US Politics

item.title: 🇺🇸 House Democrat calls Germany troop withdrawal no way to run foreign policy
item.description: Rep. Jason Crow (D-CO) criticized the Trump administration decision to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, calling it a strategic mistake amid rising European security concerns. | Source: The Hill | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇪🇸 Spanish PMs plane makes emergency landing in Turkey due to technical fault
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez official aircraft en route to Armenia made an emergency landing in the Turkish capital following a minor malfunction. The incident highlights ongoing diplomatic travel to the Caucasus region. | Source: Anadolu Agency | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇭🇰 Hong Kong IPO pipeline undeterred by Iran war market volatility
item.description: The Hong Kong Stock Exchange continues its listing boom driven by AI and tech companies despite global market volatility from the Iran war. IPO bankers elsewhere are struggling while Hong Kong remains resilient. | Source: Nikkei Asia | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🇮🇷 Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi in Critical Condition in Iranian Hospital
item.description: Jailed Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi was moved to a cardiac care unit in northwestern Iran and remains in critical condition, her family foundation reports. | Source: Reuters | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🌏 Asian Development Bank Offers Aid to Pacific Nations Hit by Iran War
item.description: The ADB announced readiness to deliver urgent assistance to Pacific countries affected by Middle East conflict and resulting fuel shortages. | Source: Reuters | Category: Economy

item.title: 🇩🇪🇺🇸 German Chancellor Downplays Rift with Trump After US Troop Drawdown
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz sought to ease tensions with Washington, stating Germany must work with the US within NATO despite policy differences. | Source: Reuters | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland Sees No Delays in US Patriot Deliveries Despite Global Pressures
item.description: Defense Minister Kosiniak-Kamysz confirmed Poland has received no signals of delays to Patriot missile system deliveries from the United States. | Source: Reuters | Category: Defense Industry

item.title: 📈 Gulf Stock Markets Rise as Iran-US Uncertainty Caps Upside
item.description: Most Gulf bourses closed higher on Sunday, but gains were limited by ongoing uncertainty over a potential US-Iran peace deal. | Source: Reuters | Category: Economy

item.title: 🚢 Ship Attacked Off Iran as Tehran Reviews US Response to Peace Proposal
item.description: A bulk carrier reported being attacked by multiple small craft off Iran, according to UKMTO, as Iran reviews a US response to its latest peace proposal aimed at ending the conflict within 30 days. | Source: Reuters | Category: US-Iran War
